free_text: Short report. No data.
Data via IAEA, Vienna.
Description
of experimental conditions.
Description
of experimental conditions.
Total neutron cross section of D(2)-O.
Democritus research reactor.
Fast chopper.
Reactor.
The sample holder consists of two aluminum plates, one
of which contains a slot which serves as the sample
space. The plates are pressed together by means of two
iron plates and bolts. Four flat heating elements are
placed in slots between the iron and aluminum plates.
A tight seal of the sample space is obtained by means
of a lead ring pressed between the aluminum plates.
Two wells are provided in the body of the aluminum
plates for measurement and control of temperature.
An identical sample holder was used as a dummy in the
sample out position in order to compensate for the
absorption of aluminum of the actual sample holder.
The thickness of the sample, was 7.3 mm., with an
error of about 0.02 mm.
Corrected on dead time
Time of flight.
A bank of four BF3 counters.
* The MAN neutron spectrometer is installed at another
horizontal neutron beam.
Total uncertainty
The errors given in the tables are the
statistical standard deviations. They were between
about 0.4%, in the thermal region and 1% at energies
higher or lower than thermal
Other sources of error have also been examined, and
were generally found to be smaller than the statistical
error.
For comparison with previous results the cross section
per hydrogen atom in water, at room temperature, has
been calculated.
Private communication from Dritsa via
IAEA, Vienna, 01/06/1971.
